  • Longer Training Times: Training deep learning models can be time-consuming due to their complexity and data requirements.
  • Lower transparency: The internal workings of deep learning models can be opaque, making it difficult to understand and interpret the decision-making processes.

 

In contrast, classical machine learning techniques:

  • Lower Data Requirements: Can perform well with smaller datasets.
  • Less Computational Demand: Typically require less computational power compared to deep learning.
  • Faster Training Times: Generally quicker to train, making them suitable for rapid development and iteration.
  • Higher Transparency: More transparent, allowing for better understanding and explanation of how the model arrives at its decisions.

 

(Reference: https://levity.ai/blog/difference-machine-learning-deep-learning, https://aws.amazon.com/what-is/deep-learning/#:~:text=Deep%20learning%20is%20a%20method,produce%20accurate%20insights%20and%20predictions, https://www.ibm.com/topics/deep-learning, https://www.techtarget.com/searchenterpriseai/definition/deep-learning-deep-neural-network)

 

What is generative AI

Definition:

Generative AI refers to a type of Artificial Intelligence that can produce new content, such as text, imagery, audio, and more, based on the data it was trained on. Unlike traditional AI systems, which primarily rely on detecting patterns in pre-existing data to perform tasks, generative AI has the unique capability to create original content that did not exist in the training data.

Context:

It's important to note that Generative AI as a field of study is separate to Deep Learning and Machine Learning however most of the new generative AI applications we see today are modelled off deep learning techniques.

 

(Reference: https://www.mckinsey.com/featured-insights/mckinsey-explainers/what-is-generative-ai, https://research.ibm.com/blog/what-is-generative-AI)

 

Large Language Models:

Definition:

A large language model (LLM) is a computer model designed for natural language processing (NLP) tasks. NLP, in this context, is a subset of artificial intelligence (AI) that aims to enable computers to understand, interpret, and generate human language. Today's LLMs primarily use deep learning techniques, although they may incorporate concepts from classical machine learning as part of their development or in combination with other approaches to enhance their capabilities.

Context:

LLMs are largely the reason for the latest boom in interest in AI through providing non-technical users a easy to consume UI that allows them to use and communicate with more complex AI technology.

(Reference: https://www.cloudflare.com/en-gb/learning/ai/what-is-large-language-model/, https://www.ibm.com/topics/large-language-models)

entry.title: From Blue Links to Direct Answers: My Personal Journey Through the AI Search Revolution
entry.content:
Search has always been a cornerstone of my studies and professional journey. It was instrumental in both my personal and career growth. I still remember the awe I felt seeing those blue links and clicking through them to find the answers I needed. The thrill of discovery, of piecing together information from various sources, shaped much of how I learned and worked. Now, the landscape has changed—those blue links are being replaced with direct answers, removing the need for all those clicks. And it’s this same sense of awe that I feel today, as we witness the emergence of a new AI-driven search market that is transforming how we interact with information.

The artificial intelligence (AI) search market is undergoing a wave of transformation, as new players take bold strides to challenge established giants like Google and Microsoft. This dynamic shift is driven by rapid advancements in AI technologies and an increasing demand for more personalized and intuitive search experiences. Apart from Google AI Overviews, these are some other notable AI Search Engines which were in news in the last couple of weeks.

Perplexity AI: A Rising Star in AI Search

One of these emerging players is Perplexity AI, an ambitious AI-powered search startup that is making significant inroads into the search landscape. Recently, the startup reported processing over 100 million search queries per week—a clear sign of growing user interest in AI-driven search solutions (TechCrunch). Unlike traditional search engines that present an array of links, Perplexity uses large language models to offer direct, concise answers in a conversational style. This approach eliminates the need for users to sift through pages of search results, providing an efficient, user-centric experience that positions Perplexity as a formidable contender in the AI search space.

Meta's Leap into AI Search

Meta Platforms Inc., too, is preparing to join the AI search revolution. The social media giant is currently developing its own AI-powered search engine, aiming to reduce its dependence on external providers like Google and Bing. Meta's search engine is designed to enhance the functionality of its AI chatbot, providing real-time responses across Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp. This move reflects Meta's ambitions to create a fully integrated search capability that serves users within its ecosystem, delivering instant answers about trending topics and events.

OpenAI's SearchGPT: Expanding Capabilities

OpenAI, a key player in the AI domain, has introduced SearchGPT—an AI-driven search engine that combines real-time data retrieval with conversational AI capabilities. SearchGPT marks an important enhancement to ChatGPT's utility, enabling users to receive up-to-date, conversational responses. This innovation positions OpenAI to compete more directly in the search market, offering an alternative that seamlessly integrates AI-driven dialogue with traditional search functions.

Changing Market Dynamics: Google's Dominance Challenged

Google has long been the dominant force in the search market, processing roughly 8.5 billion searches every day (SEMRush). Despite this overwhelming volume, the emergence of AI-driven alternatives like Perplexity AI, Meta's forthcoming search tool, and OpenAI's SearchGPT signals a shift towards more conversational and tailored search experiences. As users become more comfortable with these alternatives, we could see a redistribution of market share, particularly as these newer platforms continue to innovate.

Recent statistics underscore this shifting dynamic. As of October 2024, Google's global search engine market share stands at 89.33%, a slight decrease compared to previous years, reflecting growing competition (SEMRush). Microsoft’s Bing holds 4.15% of the market, while Yandex and Yahoo! account for 2.8% and 1.33%, respectively. In the United States, Google's market share was 86.94% in March 2024, with Bing rising to 8.04%, marking its highest recorded share in the U.S. to date (Statista). The rapid growth of AI-driven platforms, alongside increasing user comfort with these newer tools, indicates a potential shift in market dynamics as more personalized and conversational options become available.

Furthermore, the global AI market is projected to exceed $1 trillion by 2028, growing at an annual rate of 40% from 2023 to 2028 (SEMRush). This explosive growth is indicative of the increasing integration of AI technologies across various sectors, including search engines, and highlights the transformative potential of AI-driven solutions in reshaping user experiences.

Navigating Legal and Ethical Challenges

The rise of AI-powered search engines also introduces a complex set of legal and ethical issues. A key concern centers around the use of content from third-party publishers. Perplexity AI, for example, is facing legal action from major publishers such as The New York Times and News Corp, who have alleged unauthorized use of their content. These lawsuits underline the ongoing tension between the desire for innovation in AI and the need to protect intellectual property rights. Ensuring a balance between innovation and ethical practices is becoming increasingly crucial as these technologies evolve.

Below is a list summarizing notable lawsuits involving AI search platforms:

 

  1. October 2024: Dow Jones & New York Post vs. Perplexity AI - Allegation: Unauthorized use of copyrighted journalism content. Source: Wall Street Journal
  2. October 2024: The New York Times vs. Perplexity AI - Allegation: Unauthorized scraping and use of content. Source: The Verge
  3. December 2023: The New York Times vs. OpenAI & Microsoft - Allegation: Unlawful use of millions of articles to train AI models. Source: Bloomberg Law
  4. June 2023: Authors Guild vs. OpenAI - Allegation: Copyright infringement for using fiction works to train language models. Source: TechPolicy.Press
  5. June 2023: Paul Tremblay & Mona Awad vs. OpenAI - Allegation: Copyright infringement for using nonfiction works to train language models. Source: TechPolicy.Press

 

These cases highlight the ongoing tension between AI innovation and the protection of intellectual property rights. As AI search technologies continue to evolve, addressing these legal and ethical challenges remains crucial to ensure a balanced and fair digital ecosystem.

Disclaimer: This blog was written with assistance from ChatGPT, and the image used was generated using DALL-E.

Conclusion: A New Era in AI Search

The AI search market is evolving at breakneck speed, with new players introducing fresh approaches that challenge traditional search paradigms. These innovations promise to deliver enhanced user experiences—more conversational, more direct, and more efficient. However, they also bring legal and ethical considerations to the forefront, reminding us of the importance of establishing fair practices that foster a balanced digital ecosystem. As the market continues to evolve, it will be fascinating to watch how these shifts impact the future of search, and whether these rising players can truly disrupt Google's long-held dominance.

References:

  1. TechCrunch: Perplexity AI Hits 100 Million Queries

  2. SEMRush: Google Search Statistics 2024

  3. SEMRush: Search Engine Market Share 2024

  4. Statista: Leading Search Engines USA

  5. SEMRush: Global AI Market Growth 2028

  6. Wall Street Journal: Dow Jones & New York Post vs. Perplexity AI

  7. The Verge: The New York Times vs. Perplexity AI

  8. Bloomberg Law: The New York Times vs. OpenAI & Microsoft

  9. TechPolicy.Press: AI Lawsuits Overview

entry.title: SAP CodeJam HANA ML in Wrocław 2024-10 Recap
entry.content:
At the end of October, we had the Getting Started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA and Python in Wrocław, Poland, kindly hosted by LeverX and organized by @Olena_Nadtochii. 

We had a fantastic group of participants, ranging from seasoned SAP veterans...

...to fresh talents who recently graduated from university.

This SAP CodeJam took place in a historical building—the former seat of the Faculty of Pharmacy of Wrocław University: a part of the university buildings' frontage, stretching along the Odra River in the northern part of the Old Town. The view from the top was breathtaking: from the Gothic Cathedral on the right to Concordia Design on the left—the Building of the Year 2021, according to ArchDaily.

And the food was equally fabulous.

I’d like to thank all the participants who joined me for this final SAP CodeJam of 2024!

The 2024 tour included 14 stops for me, and now it has come to an end.

Now, the next stops are in 2025, and I hope to see you in Spain ( @ajmaradiaga ), UAE ( @athavanraja ), India ( @Shilpa_Shankar ). Would you like to host such an SAP CodeJam in your location? Here are the details: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/quot-getting-started-with-machine-learning-usin...

Don't delay – register today on influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 3 in 2024!

Warm regards,

Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Discover TechEd “Live” at SAPinsider EMEA 2024
entry.content:
Following hot on the heels of the hugely successful SAP TechEd Virtual event last month, attention now turns to a number of live events that will feature TechEd content and speakers, starting at the ASUG Tech Connect event in West Palm Beach, the first of SAP’s “TechEd on Tour” locations, as well as featuring in sessions at the SAPinsider EMEA “TechInsider” event in Copenhagen. Both events run from 12- 14 November in these wonderful, if quite different, locations.

I’ve been deeply involved in the planning for the SAPinsider event again, and for this reason alone will focus my blog on that event, though I am hopeful that both events will deliver a rich experience of TechEd content for attendees, and allow them to delve deeply into SAP technologies, particularly relating to SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and Artificial Intelligence (AI).

At SAPinsider Copenhagen we’re going the extra mile, in trying to deliver a TechEd-style experience for attendees, who I’m happy to say will not only find an agenda littered with TechEd sessions, delivered by many of our stellar TechEd speakers, but also they’ll be able to participate in multiple hands-on sessions as well as having an opportunity to continue their learning experience by networking with SAP’s Developer Advocates who will guide and support attendees as they work through a number of the tutorials available at the Developer Garage on the show floor – which we are pleased will make its very first appearance at this SAPinsider event.

With all this on offer I’m excited for what’s in store in Copenhagen next week – and I hope that you are too!

Plan Ahead – And Set Your Agenda Now!

For those of you registered for the event, and in a manner which is not unlike visiting a Disney theme park, my suggestion for you to get the most out of an SAPinsider event is to plan ahead – not so much to beat the queues as you might at the theme park, but more so to make sure that you see all you want to see, and achieve what you set out to do. And the best way to do this is to prepare your agenda for the event before you go.

The first step is of course to get the event app, “SAPinsider Events”, available on the App Store and Google Play, and once you’ve installed that you’re good to go in terms of adding sessions to your personal agenda. Now for the fun bit – what sessions to see? Perhaps I might help with some ideas.

I’ll assume that everyone will plan to join the day 1 and day 2 keynotes. I’m looking forward to both, the motivational first day keynote from Hein Wagner, starting at 9.00am on Tuesday 12th November, followed the next day by the AI inspired second day keynote, featuring Martin Böhm of Ottobock and Jonathan Von Rüden of SAP, and which starts at the same time of day on the 13th November.

But after the keynotes, what next? Here are my personal 6 TechEd inspired picks for Copenhagen:

  1. Enhancing SAP Development Processes with SAP Build and ABAP Cloud Interoperability to Elevate Your Extensions, 12-Nov-24, 10:30 - 11:15
  2. Hands-On Lab: Develop Applications and Processes with SAP Build and Joule, 12-Nov-24, 15:30 - 17:30
  3. Mastering SAP Business AI for Enhanced Performance and Innovation, 13-Nov-24, 10:30 - 11:15
  4. SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere Solutions: Integration, 13-Nov-24, 14:15 - 15:00
  5. Clean Core Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, 13-Nov-24, 16:45 - 17:30
  6. SAP BTP Strategy and Highlights, 14-Nov-24, 09:00 - 09:45

Of course, this is just the tip of the iceberg, as there are many more sessions available for you to select from the agenda, and among which you’ll find more TechEd content, as well as content from other speakers from SAP and our business partners, in addition to many customer case stories too. And in a shameless plug too, you may also note my session “Navigating Your Path to Success with SAP BTP” which I shall deliver alongside Nis Boy Naeve on 13 November at 11:30am. Please join us if you can! Take a look at the SAPinsider event website sessions list, or the event app that you’ve already downloaded to see more.

Your Continued Learning Experience

If you’d like to continue your own personal learning journey after the event, then why not explore other learning opportunities from SAP? For example, learn how to build software applications, side-by-side extensions, and integrations to and from cloud applications by exploring SAP’s free learning content on SAP BTP. It is made for both integration designers and extension developers from all levels of expertise and will help you stay up to date with the latest SAP BTP innovations. And check out even more role-based learning resources and opportunities to get certified in one place on SAP Learning site.

Or if you’re interested in TechEd sessions, but can’t join the events in West Palm Beach or Copenhagen, then perhaps another TechEd on Tour location, in Australia, Germany or the UK would work better for you?

For those of you travelling to Copenhagen next week, I do hope that you find SAPinsider EMEA 2024 to be a truly informative and stimulating experience. And, of course, if you visit the show floor then look me up at the SAP exhibit booth, and I’ll be happy to have a chat with you about SAP BTP, the conference or just about the amazing city of Copenhagen!

My best wishes for safe travels, and I hope that you enjoy the event!

Chris Grundy

Product Marketing, SAP BTP

entry.title: Unlocking the Power of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) in AI: A Game Changer for SAP Customers
entry.content:
The rapid progression in artificial intelligence (AI), especially with large language models (LLMs), is transforming how businesses approach intelligent decision-making. Yet, as promising as these technologies are, challenges such as hallucination—where AI generates incorrect or irrelevant information—can limit their effectiveness in real-world business applications.

One of the key SAP innovations overcoming this issue is Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). By combining LLMs with real-time data retrieval, RAG provides a powerful solution for generating reliable, accurate insights.

In this blog, we’ll dive into RAG, its importance, and how SAP is empowering its customers to make the most of this cutting-edge technology.

What Exactly is Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)?

RAG offers a breakthrough by blending generative AI models with a dynamic data retrieval process. When posed with a query, the AI doesn’t just rely on pre-trained knowledge. Instead, it actively fetches the most relevant, up-to-date information from databases or document repositories, resulting in responses that are both accurate and contextually grounded.

This hybrid approach greatly reduces the risks of hallucinations and ensures that the AI’s outputs are not only trustworthy but also highly applicable to real-time business scenarios.

The Two Pillars of RAG:

  • Retrieval: This component involves the model retrieving the most pertinent data or documents in response to a query.
  • Generation: Using that retrieved information, the model generates a coherent and contextually relevant answer.

By integrating real-time data into the generation process, businesses can ensure that their AI systems provide insightful, accurate, and timely responses.

Why Do We Need RAG?

While LLMs have revolutionized the use of AI for deriving insights, their limitations in practical business applications are well-known:

  • Hallucinations: LLMs may present factually inaccurate or irrelevant responses when detached from current data.
  • Cost of Fine-Tuning: Customizing models with domain-specific knowledge requires significant time, effort, and cost.
  • Stagnant Knowledge: Once trained, LLMs don’t automatically update, limiting their value in fast-paced industries.

By embedding a retrieval mechanism, RAG addresses these limitations. It ensures that AI-generated responses are not only relevant but also backed by the most recent and accurate information available.

Why is RAG Crucial for SAP’s Customers?

Industries such as supply chain management, human resources, and customer relationship management, where SAP operates, thrive on accurate, up-to-date information. Implementing RAG within these sectors offers:

  • Reliable insights: By grounding AI outputs in the latest business data, responses are more trustworthy and useful.
  • Faster response times: RAG enables AI-driven systems to pull relevant information quickly, streamlining business operations.
  • Cost efficiency: Businesses can use RAG with existing data repositories instead of engaging in costly LLM fine-tuning.

SAP’s Approach to RAG

SAP is actively incorporating RAG into its platform, providing users with the tools to build AI systems tailored to their specific needs. Here’s how SAP is enabling this:

  • Centralized RAG Service: SAP’s scalable, domain-agnostic RAG service integrates smoothly with existing user interfaces. This service ensures that AI models leverage the most up-to-date information while also offering transparency by tracing the sources of data used in responses.
  • Vector Search Engine in SAP HANA Cloud: SAP’s advanced vector engine efficiently stores and queries data embeddings, making retrieval faster and more efficient.

Document Grounding in SAP’s Joule and AI Services

SAP’s AI services, including Joule—an AI copilot—are enhancing their capabilities through RAG:

  • Indexing & Retrieval: Documents are transformed into vector embeddings, which are stored and retrieved for accurate, contextual responses.
  • Grounded Generation: The retrieved data informs AI-generated answers, significantly improving accuracy and relevance.

Real-World Impact: RAG in SAP SuccessFactors

One of the most promising applications of RAG within SAP’s ecosystem is in the realm of HR, specifically within SAP SuccessFactors. Typically, answering HR-related queries could take up to 20 minutes per question, putting a strain on HR teams.

With RAG integrated into tools like Joule, employees can now access relevant policies instantly. By querying policy documents directly, the system retrieves relevant sections, generating accurate answers in seconds. This has led to a 35% reduction in the volume of HR inquiries.

Conclusion

The introduction of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) is revolutionizing how businesses can harness AI for more accurate, grounded, and reliable insights.

SAP’s integration of this technology into platforms like Joule and BTP allows its customers to take full advantage of AI-driven decision-making without the high costs and complexity of fine-tuning models. As a result, SAP customers are poised to unlock significant value by adopting RAG in their day-to-day operations.

entry.title: Join Our Espresso Session: Unlocking the Potential of GenAI in the Utilities Industry on November 19
entry.content:
Dear Utilities Community,

The utility industry is currently undergoing a significant transformation through the use of artificial intelligence. 

AI will have a massive impact on the business processes of utility companies, enabling unprecedented efficiency gains in processes and offering users of IT systems an unprecedented user experience. The consistent decentralization of energy generation, which is of central importance for a sustainable energy transition, will hardly be possible without AI-supported technologies. GenAI will also fundamentally change the way energy suppliers interact with their customers, revolutionizing customer service.

We are delighted to announce our upcoming webcast "Unlocking the Potential of GenAI in the Utilities Industry" on Tuesday, November 19 from 4pm - 4:30pm CET. 

During this session, you will:

  • Explore AI-powered scenarios and use cases across various business functions
  • Learn about SAP solutions with AI capabilities

 

Speakers: 

Stefan Engelhardt: VP Industry Business Unit Utilities at SAP

Bruno Pincolini: Business Development for Utilities at SAP

Please join us next week and register here 

See you there!

entry.title: ASUG Tech Connect 2024 | Keynote Highlights & Resources
entry.content:
Today, the Americas’ SAP Users’ Group (ASUG) hosted the  first SAP TechEd on Tour stop in Florida! Together with Geoff Scott, CEO of ASUG, and Patrick Dineen, SVP Finance Transformation Systems at NBC Universal, we explored how SAP is helping organizations accelerate ERP modernization and drive innovation across industries with SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP), SAP Joule, SAP Build, and SAP Integration Suite. 

In this blog post, I've compiled all relevant links and demos presented during my segment of the keynote – and more!  

If you want to learn more about SAP TechEd on Tour and other stops, go to https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html.  

 

Best of TechEd – Virtual keynote recap  

The main SAP Business Technology Platform announcements from our virtual keynote focused on three key points: 

  • First: We help customers get faster, better, and automated: SAP BTP serves all their needs to innovate, integrate, and extend their systems.  
  • Second: We are enhancing the developer and user experience with our latest release of SAP Build, which now includes ABAP Cloud.   
  • And third: We are raising the bar for Business AI – for a whole new level of productivity.  

You will find all announcements shared at SAP TechEd 2024 in our News Guide and the replay of our opening keynote. 

 

Best of Platform 

SAP BTP is your platform of choice for application development, automation, integration, data & analytics, and Business AI. 

To support your journey with BTP, we provide a wide range of tools, best practices, resources, and reference architectures designed to help you:

  • Design and deploy enterprise-grade solutions,
  • accelerate time-to-value and optimize costs, and
  • foster collaboration and continuous learning in your architectural practices.

How to get started? At ASUG TechConnect, I highlighted some of the key tools and resources available to get you started effectively:

The SAP BTP Guidance Framework helps you design solutions that align with your company’s digital strategy. You can find more information here on SAP Help, and BTP reference architectures at the SAP Discovery Center. Adopt and adjust the architectures to your requirements by using the BTP solution diagram repository.

Or simply tune into our SAP TechEd session on demand:

  • Start your journey with SAP BTP and confidently accelerate adoption

The SAP Discovery Center provides you with hands-on guidance to implement BTP services  based on real use cases! You can activate them in your BTP landscape with the new quick account setup. With a single click you will get all services provisioned and deployed. Learn more about this in the respective SAP TechEd on-demand sessions:

  • Get started with SAP BTP: Discover and accelerate with SAP Discovery Center
  • Modernize your technology landscape with SAP BTP and SAP LeanIX solutions

The SAP Business Accelerator Hub equips you with additional building blocks to realize your digital strategy. More than 25,000 pre-built assets are already available, such as Integration flows, APIs, Business Processes, adapters, validated partner use cases and a lot more.

The SAP BTP GenAI starter kit empowers you to integrate AI capabilities into your infrastructure easily. Get a first overview in this blog post and more detailed information here on GitHub.

Once you’re set up with BTP, a world of possibilities opens up. In our keynote, I highlighted why BTP stands as the premier platform for embedding AI, accelerating developer productivity, building applications, and seamlessly integrating SAP with third-party solutions:

AI Foundation on BTP
The AI Foundation on BTP helps you make the most out of your SAP data and processes. You also get the broadest selection of foundational models from top providers—all in one place. Find out more through our SAP TechEd on-demand sessions:

  • Development with our generative AI hub, SDKs, and orchestration features
  • Enable more secure and responsible generative AI

Joule and Joule studio
By year-end, our AI copilot Joule will cover 80% of SAP's top workflows, enabling users to manage timesheets, view returns, create reports, and compare quotes through natural language. Integrated with SAP Build, it also boosts developer productivity. Discover additional insights in the following SAP TechEd on-demand session:

  • Extend Joule with SAP Build and develop custom enterprise skills

Joule Agents
Joule is going agentic! Powered by large language models, AI agents tackle complex business problems through planning, self-reflection, reasoning, and collaboration.

SAP Build x ABAP
Two years ago, we launched SAP Build as a unified low-code environment. Last year, we added SAP Build Code for AI-driven app development. Now, SAP Build also integrates directly with SAP S/4HANA, enabling BAPI and function module calls in the S/4HANA Private Cloud. And we now also included ABAP Cloud in our latest release of SAP Build!

We also introduced the new ABAP explain agent, allowing users to quickly understand complex ABAP code by generating explanations through large language models. Learn more in our TechEd on-demand sessions: 

  • Enhance your fusion team collaboration with ABAP Cloud and SAP Build
  • Boost your coding efficiency: Explore Joule's ABAP developer capabilities

Integration Suite
With SAP Integration Suite, we offer you a modern solution to run your integration scenarios across SAP and third-party applications. You benefit from embedded AI, hundreds of connectors, and thousands of pre-built integrations. Already 75% of our customers use SAP Integration Suite to integrate with non-SAP systems. And we are introducing new integration adapters for Anaplan, Coupa, HubSpot, NetSuite ERP, Snowflake, and more.

  • Innovate now with a modern, AI-assisted integration suite

 

Best of Suite

With SAP BTP powering our suite of enterprise applications, we also explored how everything connects across our portfolio. A suite-first approach ensures a seamless, productive user experience throughout the workday.

As an example, we presented a demo that shows how the end users of our products can easily navigate across our solution portfolio via SAP Start. Infused by our copilot Joule, users can be more productive when setting up new teams, booking travel, or getting insights through SAP Analytics Cloud, for example. You can watch the full demo here.

  •  View our Integration Strategy Paper

 

Get started now!

To get started with BTP and Business AI, here are some of our missions in the SAP Discovery Center:

  • AI on BTP: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/4338/4621/
  • GenAI with SAP Build Code: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/4441/4727/
  • Gen AI with RAG for CAP: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/4371/4655/ 

Here are some additional missions to kick off your journey with BTP: 

  • App Development on BTP: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/4431/4717/
  • Integration Suite: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/3258/3327/
  • Best Of Suite: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/4451/4737/

I hope you are all fired up now and ready to get your hands dirty.

We can’t wait to see what you will build!

entry.title: Upcoming Webinar: HR and the Role of AI in 2025
entry.content:
How many times have you heard someone say “2024 is the year of AI”? It is perhaps one of the most ubiquitous statements of the year – and for good reason. When it comes to the evolution of AI, 2024 did not disappoint. What once was just a lofty vision or a go-to buzzword, AI has quickly become a reality for many of us in our day-to-day lives. And while its effects have been far-reaching, many organizational leaders still face uncertainty and confusion around the best way to bring AI into their business processes. With 2025 just around the corner, now is the perfect time to sort through all the noise and evaluate your AI strategy, especially as it relates to HR.

To help support your planning processes, we’re thrilled to be hosting a live webinar on the topic of bringing AI to HR. This is a great opportunity to hear directly from leading experts and to ask your most pressing questions. During the session, we’ll cover the following:

  • The latest research findings on how AI is changing the world of work
  • How businesses are responding to the latest market trends
  • SAP’s AI strategy and how it is impacting HR and the rest of the organization

Additionally, you’ll get to see firsthand how the latest AI-enabled innovations can drive meaningful results across the organization with a live product demo featuring Joule, an AI copilot.

Click here to register for the live event on November 21st at 11 AM Eastern

We look forward to seeing you there!

entry.title: Updated Learning Journey - Exploring the Lead to Cash Business Process in SAP Customer Experience
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community! Fernando at the controls!

Just two years ago in November, we released the Exploring the Lead to Cash Business Process in SAP Customer Experience learning journey.

After about 4,000 learners going over it and more than 1,000 record of achievement badges issued to those who have completed the whole course, we analyzed the feedback given to us, and started a mayor update of the whole course content.

This complete course update comes with a long number of changes:

  • Updated SAP Customer Experience Solutions Portfolio used during the business process.

 

  • Introduced Artificial Intelligence for CX and its use cases during the stages for lead to cash supported by all Customer Experience solutions.

 

  • Included a Business Sample to during all the Lead to Cash stages to visualize the steps.

 

  • Updated the Lead to Cash Process Variants with those already mentioned in SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

 

  • 10 net new units and lessons for all the Lead to Cash content with new videos, diagrams, text descriptions and further learning resources.

So, what are you waiting for? go to SAP Learning site, and enroll for the new updated version of Exploring the Lead to Cash Business Process in SAP Customer Experience, after completing it... Don't forget to claim your digital badge to show it in your professional social network profile!

I’ll be back very soon to present you new learning courses, live sessions, micro learnings and any other educational asset which can be interesting for you related to SAP Customer Experience Integration and Extensibility area!

In the mean time, I’ll be looking forward to your feedback and questions via comments here in this post.

entry.title: SAP MaxAttention Summit: Uniting Thought Leaders for Success
entry.content:
The Summit is scheduled for April 13-15, 2025

We are excited to announce that the SAP MaxAttention Summit is taking place in Munich, Germany, from April 13-15, 2025. This exclusive event is an onsite, in-person gathering designed for the SAP MaxAttention community to engage with thought leaders, SAP experts, and peers from some of the world's leading organizations.

What to expect

From Sunday evening through Tuesday afternoon, the summit will feature captivating keynotes from SAP executives, customer experience sessions, and in-depth expert discussions. In addition, there will be a variety of networking opportunities, including a welcome reception, a special evening event, and one-on-one meetings.

  • Take the opportunity to connect with members of the SAP Executive Board and engage in in-depth expert discussions.
  • Participants will be able to experience SAP MaxAttention in action and learn how leading companies within the SAP MaxAttention community establish their individual North Star enterprise architecture, transition to RISE with SAP, and integrate innovations like SAP Business AI.
  • Moreover, the summit provides you with a chance to connect with a select group of SAP's largest customers, to build relationships, and to gain valuable insights that can take your business to the next level.

Event Location

Welcome Reception (Sunday, April 13, 2025)

Take a look at our booklet 

Check out our booklet including more information around the location, key topics and agenda now. 

Customers eligible to join this exclusive SAP MaxAttention event will be invited personally in January.  

We can't wait to see you in Munich next April for what promises to be an unforgettable experience. Let's come together to learn, connect, and drive success! 

In case of questions, please don’t hesitate to reach out.

entry.title: Dive into the World of AI with SAP Learning to Future-Proof Your Career
entry.content:
AI is not just a buzzword but a transformative force across industries. From predictive analytics and automation to enhancing customer experiences and operational efficiencies, AI is reshaping the business world. For companies and professionals eager to stay at the forefront, understanding AI's principles and applications is indispensable. Fortunately, SAP Learning offers an array of resources designed to help you stay ahead of the curve and enhance your professional expertise.

 

 

Why Choose SAP Learning?
SAP Learning provides extensive courses tailored for both beginners and seasoned tech professionals. Join the community of 2 million learners and get insights from industry experts in both practical and theoretical aspects of AI. With flexible online courses designed to fit your busy schedule, you can study at your own pace and deepen your knowledge and skills efficiently.

 

Key Learning Journeys to Explore:

  1. Putting AI Ethics into Practice at SAP: Begin your journey by understanding the importance of AI ethics and learn how to implement ethical standards in AI development. This course ensures you grasp the crucial role of ethical considerations in the deployment of AI technologies.
  2. Exploring the SAP Business AI Portfolio: Before diving into specific tools, ensure you complete this prerequisite course. It offers essential insights into SAP's AI offerings, laying a solid foundation for further specialized learning.
  3. Provisioning and Implementing Joule: Gain hands-on experience with one of SAP's powerful AI tools. This journey walks you through the entire process of leveraging Joule to streamline business operations, providing practical, real-world skills you can apply immediately.

 

Become Part of the Intelligent Future
The future is intelligent, and being an integral part of this transformation starts with acquiring the right skills. Equip yourself with the knowledge offered on the SAP Learning site. Embrace the AI-driven transformation in the business world and become a valuable asset in any organization's AI endeavors.

 

The future is intelligent. Be a part of it with SAP.
Explore SAP Learning today, and take the first step towards future-proofing your career.
